ip_outcome_note: Card name, National Pokedex no. 337, crescent-moon body with single red eye, Hoenn Rock/Psychic type — all match Lunatone canonical design unambiguously. PokemonTCG.io confirms card name 'Lunatone' directly.

trivia_bullets.text: Burning Shadows (SM3) released August 5, 2017, in the Sun & Moon series — 177 cards total. The set name references the shadow-organization Team Skull and the Ultra Beasts threatening Alola. [PokemonTCG.io: set swsh3]

Lunatone carries National Pokedex no. 337. It was introduced in Generation III (Ruby/Sapphire, 2002) and classified as the 'Meteorite Pokemon,' a Rock/Psychic type discovered at a location where a meteorite fell roughly 40 years before the events of the games. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia via snippet]

Lunatone was version-exclusive to Pokemon Sapphire (found in Meteor Falls), while its counterpart Solrock was Ruby-exclusive. Both share the 'Meteorite Pokemon' species classification, encoding the sun/moon version duality into a single lore origin. [WebSearch: pokemondb.net / Bulbapedia snippet]

This card's Heal Block Ability ('If you have Solrock in play, Pokemon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be healed') is mechanically paired with Solrock's reciprocal Ability — each card requires its counterpart to activate, encoding the version-counterpart bond directly into gameplay. [PokemonTCG.io: sm3-68]
'Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.' [PokemonTCG.io: sm3-68]

related_cards.name: Solrock
related_cards.note: Mechanically paired counterpart — Heal Block and Resistance Shade Abilities each require the other to be in play; Ruby-version counterpart to Lunatone's Sapphire exclusivity

related_cards.name: Lunatone
related_cards.note: Multiple printings across the TCG; BUS-68 is the Sun & Moon era version
